description: Interval in seconds between line-of-sight update ticks for human-type NPCs tracking known player positions
Interval in seconds between line-of-sight update ticks for all NPC types tracking known player positions
Interval in seconds between full AI brain senses update ticks; controls how frequently NPCs refresh their awareness of surroundings
How many milliseconds to spend on ammo updating per frame
How many milliseconds to spend on target scanning per frame
How many milliseconds to spend on a tick per frame
When enabled, logs debug information about AI ejection events when passengers are removed from boat seats
When enabled, boats without nearby players will slowly drift toward the shore after the shore drift delay elapses
When enabled, procedural patrol paths are generated for boats at server startup; false in editor to skip path generation during testing
When enabled, all fishing attempts fail immediately; cheat for testing failed-catch animations and UI feedback
When enabled, all fishing attempts succeed immediately regardless of bite probability; cheat for testing fishing catch logic
When enabled, fish bite the hook immediately after casting without any wait time; cheat for testing bite response
Interval in seconds between long-range detectability checks for buried objects; longer interval saves CPU for distant searches
Minimum distance in metres the player must move before a new long-range detection check is triggered
Interval in seconds between nearest-detectable-object distance checks performed by the metal detector
Minimum time (s) between revalidating individual missions via updateMissionValidStateWorkQueue
When enabled, missions are available and can be assigned to players; disable to globally suppress mission generation and assignment on the server
How long per frame (ms) to spend processing updateMissionValidStateWorkQueue
Minimum time (s) between starting runs of updateMissionValidStateWorkQueue
Toggles the usage of mountable MountedPlayerSync optimisations (only used by boat scientists currently)
How many frames between base navigation movement updates
The max step-up height difference for pet base navigation
How long we are not moving for before trigger the stuck event
When enabled, server occlusion is taken into account when updating player relationship visibility data; saved between restarts
Per-frame CPU budget in milliseconds for the bot collider work queue that updates NPC physics colliders
Per-frame CPU budget in milliseconds for processing the player relationship (contacts/team) update queue
How many minutes before a siege weapon loses all its health while outside
How long before a submarine loses all its health while in deep water
How long before a submarine loses all its health while outside. If it's in deep water, deepwaterdecayminutes is used
How long a submarine can stay underwater until players start taking damage from low oxygen
Maximum building block upgrade grade (0=twig,1=wood,2=stone,3=metal,4=top tier) that the battering ram can damage; default 2 (stone)
Population active on the server, per square km
How long before a Beehive will update
How long before the Beehive will perform temperature and inside checks
How much the Nucleus's XP should be increased per honeycomb generated
Range to leave current target alone (should be higher than search)
How far away fire has to be to set the swarm on fire
How long a swarm will stick around without a target
Range to find new targets
How many milliseconds to spend on thinking per frame
How much water a player needs to be in to be ignored
How many child swarms a master swarm will create
How long a master swarm will stick around without a target
How long before a master swarm will create a child
Can bike crashes cause damage or death to the rider?
Motorbike population in monuments
How long before a bike loses all its health while outside
Pedal bike population in monuments
Pedal bike population active on the server (roadside spawns)
Amount of collision damage on a bike required to ragdoll the player
Maximum initial bet per round
Population active on the server, per square km
When enabled, boat AI entities can enter a sleep state when no players are nearby; reduce CPU usage for idle boats
How often to update the avoidance cache. Lower number means a more accurate cache at the expensive of performance.
How long per frame to spend on boat ai
When enabled, draws DDraw visualisations of boat AI steering, avoidance, and pathfinding state
Distance players need to be to start syncing mounted seats
Maximum speed as a fraction of the boat's top speed that AI-controlled boats will use; default 0.9; saved and shown in admin UI
When enabled, logs verbose boat AI decision-making output to the server console each AI tick
Number of seconds a boat AI will wait without player interaction before entering sleep mode; default 30s
When enabled, damage dealt to a building block attached to a boat is forwarded up to the parent boat entity
Multiplied by the base BuildingBlockDecay duration
Multiplied by the base BuildingBlockDecay duration
Number of seconds of audio backtrack buffer maintained by the boombox for streaming synchronisation; default 30s
A list of radio stations that are valid on this server. Format: NAME,URL,NAME,URL,etc
When enabled, buoyancy point physics updates are batched together each fixed update for better CPU efficiency
Metal detector loot weight is 100.
Time in seconds before an item expires.
Allows mounting cannons outside of boats for testing.
Population active on the server, per square km
distance cloth will simulate until
Duration in seconds a player is locked out from attempting the code lock after exceeding maxFailedAttempts; default 900s (15 minutes)
Maximum number of failed code entry attempts on a code lock before the player is locked out; default 8
Can command blocks execute commands
Print a log message when a command block is executed
If enabled, commands from command blocks will run using the last player who set them, allowing for a wider range of commands to be used
Per-frame CPU budget in milliseconds for completing pending companion server camera renders
When enabled, the companion server camera rendering system is active and processes camera render requests from the companion app
Maximum age in frames for a known collider entity entry in the companion server camera cache before it is evicted
Maximum distance in metres from the companion server camera at which entity colliders are tracked for rendering
Far clipping plane distance in metres for companion server camera renders; default 250
Height in pixels of the companion server camera render output; default 90
Physics layer mask used for raycasting in companion server camera depth sampling; defaults to solid, water, and player movement layers
Maximum number of raycasts per frame used for companion server camera depth sampling
Maximum number of camera render tasks that can complete per frame for companion server cameras
Near clipping plane distance for companion server camera renders; 0 = use default
Maximum distance in metres at which player entities are included in companion server camera renders
Maximum distance in metres at which player name labels are included in companion server camera render output
Interval in seconds between successive companion server camera render dispatches; default 0.05s (20 Hz)
Number of raycast samples taken per companion server camera render pass for depth reconstruction
Vertical field of view in degrees for companion server camera renders; default 65
Width in pixels of the companion server camera render output; default 160
Controls whether the in-game admin UI is displayed to admins
Include bots in the admin UI player list (debugging purpose only)
When enabled, vision distance checks use per-bone raycasts for accuracy; disable to use a single origin ray for performance
The angle under which the AI will think it's being aimed at at by a player
If animal_ignore_food is true, animals will not sense food sources or interact with them (server optimization). (default: true)
Default network interpolation delay in seconds applied to NPC entity movement; lower values reduce visual lag at the cost of jitter on unstable connections
When enabled, AI weapons deal real damage when fired; disable to make NPC weapons harmless for testing AI behaviour safely
Target frame time budget in seconds for AI tick processing; default is 5s in production (1s in editor) to cap CPU usage per AI frame
When enabled, AI group logic is active allowing NPCs to coordinate as squads; disable to make all NPCs act as independent individuals
When enabled, AI entities ignore player presence and will not target or react to players; useful for building/testing without NPC interference
When enabled, logs AI-related warnings and issues to the server console; useful for diagnosing pathfinding and brain errors
When enabled, AI entities move toward their NavMesh destinations; disable to freeze NPC movement while keeping brain logic running
The height of the carve volume. (default: 2)
The minimum size we allow a carving volume to be. (default: 2)
The minimum number of building blocks a building needs to consist of for this optimization to be applied. (default: 25)
The size multiplier applied to the size of the carve volume. The smaller the value, the tighter the skirt around foundation edges, but too small and animals can attack through walls. (default: 4)
If nav_carve_use_building_optimization is true, we attempt to reduce the amount of navmesh carves for a building. (default: false)
When enabled, AI entities update their NavMesh agent destinations each tick; disable to freeze AI movement while keeping brain logic running
npc_alertness_drain_rate define the rate at which we drain the alertness level of an NPC when there are no enemies in sight. (Default: 0.01)
This is multiplied with the current alertness (0-10) to decide how long it will take for the NPC to deliberately miss again. (default: 0.33)
npc_alertness_zero_detection_mod define the threshold of visibility required to detect an enemy when alertness is zero. (Default: 0.5)
When enabled, NPC spawn points are validated to ensure they are on a valid NavMesh position before spawning; prevents NPCs from spawning in unreachable locations
npc_cover_compromised_cooldown defines how long a cover point is marked as compromised before it's cleared again for selection. (default: 10)
The rate at which we gather information about available cover points. Minimum value is 1, as it multiplies with the tick-rate of the fixed AI tick rate of 0.1 (Default: 20)
npc_cover_path_vs_straight_dist_max_diff defines what the maximum difference between straight-line distance and path distance can be when evaluating cover points. (default: 2)
If npc_cover_use_path_distance is set to true then npcs will look at the distance between the cover point and their target using the path between the two, rather than the straight-line distance.
The percentage away from a maximum miss the randomizer is allowed to travel when shooting to deliberately hit the target (we don't want perfect hits with every shot). (default: 0.85f)
The offset with which the NPC will maximum miss the target. (default: 1.25)
The time it takes for the NPC to deliberately miss to the time the NPC tries to hit its target. (default: 1.5)
npc_door_trigger_size defines the size of the trigger box on doors that opens the door as npcs walk close to it (default: 1.5)
If npc_enable is set to false then npcs won't spawn. (default: true)
If npc_families_no_hurt is true, npcs of the same family won't be able to hurt each other. (default: true)
The modifier by which a silencer reduce the noise that a gun makes when shot. (Default: 0.15)
Baseline damage modifier for the new HTN Player NPCs to nerf their damage compared to the old NPCs. (default: 1.15f)
npc_htn_player_frustration_threshold defines where the frustration threshold for NPCs go, where they have the opportunity to change to a more aggressive tactic. (default: 3)
If npc_ignore_chairs is true, npcs won't care about seeking out and sitting in chairs. (default: true)
npc_junkpile_dist_aggro_gate define at what range (or closer) a junkpile scientist will get aggressive. (Default: 8)
defines the chance for scientists to spawn at NPC junkpiles. (Default: 0.1)
npc_max_junkpile_count define how many npcs can spawn into the world at junkpiles at the same time (does not include monuments) (Default: 30)
npc_max_population_military_tunnels defines the size of the npc population at military tunnels. (default: 3)
This is multiplied with the max roam range stat of an NPC to determine how far from its spawn point the NPC is allowed to roam. (default: 3)
If npc_only_hurt_active_target_in_safezone is true, npcs won't any player other than their actively targeted player when in a safe zone. (default: true)
npc_patrol_point_cooldown defines the cooldown time on a patrol point until it's available again (default: 5)
The rate at which we tick the reasoning system. Minimum value is 1, as it multiplies with the tick-rate of the fixed AI tick rate of 0.1 (Default: 1)
npc_respawn_delay_max_military_tunnels defines the maximum delay between spawn ticks at military tunnels. (default: 1920)
npc_respawn_delay_min_military_tunnels defines the minimum delay between spawn ticks at military tunnels. (default: 480)
The rate at which we tick the sensory system. Minimum value is 1, as it multiplies with the tick-rate of the fixed AI tick rate of 0.1 (Default: 5)
Spawn NPCs on the Cargo Ship. (default: true)
Spawn NPCs on deep sea islands (default: false)
Spawn NPCs on junkpiles (default: true)
npc_spawn_per_tick_max_military_tunnels defines how many can maximum spawn at once at military tunnels. (default: 1)
npc_spawn_per_tick_min_military_tunnels defineshow many will minimum spawn at once at military tunnels. (default: 1)
npc_speed_crouch_run define the speed of an npc when in the crouched run state, and should be a number between 0 and 1. (Default: 0.25)
npc_speed_walk define the speed of an npc when in the crouched walk state, and should be a number between 0 and 1. (Default: 0.1)
npc_speed_walk define the speed of an npc when in the run state, and should be a number between 0 and 1. (Default: 0.4)
npc_speed_walk define the speed of an npc when in the sprint state, and should be a number between 0 and 1. (Default: 1.0)
npc_speed_walk define the speed of an npc when in the walk state, and should be a number between 0 and 1. (Default: 0.18)
If npc_use_new_aim_system is true, npcs will miss on purpose on occasion, where the old system would randomize aim cone. (default: true)
If npc_use_thrown_weapons is true, npcs will throw grenades, etc. This is an experimental feature. (default: true)
npc_valid_aim_cone defines how close their aim needs to be on target in order to fire. (default: 0.8)
npc_valid_mounted_aim_cone defines how close their aim needs to be on target in order to fire while mounted. (default: 0.92)
Global health multiplier applied to all NPCs; 1.0 = normal, 2.0 = double health; useful for difficulty scaling without modifying individual NPC prefabs
When enabled, NPCs can enter and swim in water; disable to prevent all NPCs from entering water bodies
Maximum number of path-finding iterations used when calculating NPC ocean patrol routes; higher values produce better paths at more CPU cost
Do any kind of scientists spawn on the map (default: true)
How frequently in seconds the NPC sensory system updates its awareness of nearby entities; higher values reduce CPU cost but make NPCs slower to react
When enabled, NavMesh destinations are sampled to the nearest valid NavMesh position before being set; prevents NPCs getting stuck off-mesh
When enabled, AI information zones can sleep inactive NPCs and wake them when players approach; disable to keep all NPCs awake at all times
Radius in metres within which a thrown smoke grenade suppresses NPC vision and targeting
When enabled, AI updates are spliced across multiple frames to spread CPU cost; disable to run all AI updates synchronously every tick
When enabled, AI entities run their brain Think() logic each tick; disable to freeze all AI decision-making while leaving entities in place
Number of AI think ticks per second; default is 5 (every 200ms); higher values make AI more responsive at higher CPU cost
When enabled, NavMesh path calculation uses the full CalculatePath API; disable to use the simpler SetDestination fallback only
When enabled, AI uses the spatial grid for entity queries; disable to fall back to brute-force entity iteration for debugging spatial query issues
When enabled, falls back to SetDestination if CalculatePath fails to find a valid path to the target
The angle under which the AI will think it's being watched by another entity
Should explosives parented to vehicles damage deployables
Cooldown time before alarms can send another notification (in seconds)
IP address the server listens on for incoming connections; leave empty to bind to all available network interfaces
When enabled, server-side C# exceptions are written to the server log file; disabling reduces log noise on servers with known non-critical exceptions
Maximum number of simultaneous player connections allowed; connections above this limit are rejected with a server full message
Maximum simultaneous connections from the same IP address; prevents a single host from consuming all connection slots
Maximum allowed size in bytes of a single network message; oversized messages are dropped to prevent memory exhaustion attacks
Enables sending push notifications
UDP port number the server listens on; default is 28015; must be open in firewall for players to connect
Public IP address advertised to the Steam server browser; leave empty to auto-detect; set explicitly if behind NAT
Max number of queued messages - set to 0 to disable message processing
Disables updating entirely - emergency use only
Verbosity level for static batching debug output; 0 = off, higher values print more detail about batch operations to the console
When false, prevents the Bradley APC from spawning or respawning on the server
Number of minutes after the Bradley APC is destroyed before it respawns at its patrol path; default is 60 minutes
Random variance multiplier applied to respawnDelayMinutes; a value of 1.0 means the actual delay is randomly chosen between 0 and respawnDelayMinutes
When enabled, this system is globally active; disable to deactivate the system for the current session
Number of messages to keep in memory for chat history
Maximum distance in metres within which local chat messages are visible to nearby players; messages from beyond this range are not received
When enabled, all chat messages are written to the server log file in addition to being broadcast to players
If enabled then players will need to be near a Clan Table to make changes to clans
Enables the clan system if set to true (must be set at boot, requires restart)
Maximum number of members each clan can have (local backend only!)

How much score players earn for destroying other player's tool cupboards
How much score players earn for hacking crates

How much score players earn for killing unarmed players (this value should be negative)

When enabled, all crafting completes instantly with no time delay; useful for testing crafting recipes or quickly equipping items in development
Apply creative mode to the entire server

When enabled, validates that trigger colliders are correctly parented to their entities during physics updates; helps catch mis-parenting bugs
When enabled, validates trigger collider configurations each physics update to catch incorrectly parented or sized trigger volumes
Shows debug info for what objects are causing clipping checks to fail.
Do not damage any items
When true, nav mesh obstacle components on loot containers are disabled in the deep sea zone to improve performance in underwater areas
When enabled, Unity Debug.Log output is written to disk; disabling first logs a final message before suppressing further output
When enabled, logs mission NPC speech info (speaker, line, trigger) to the console as mission dialogue events fire
Multiplier applied to all puzzle reset timers; values below 1.0 make puzzles reset faster, above 1.0 slower

Allow all vehicles to travel to the deep sea, unless of just the whitelisted vehicles

When enabled, logs each deep sea portal spawn attempt to the console including whether it succeeded or failed; useful for diagnosing portal placement issues
Toggles the deep sea. Needs a server restart to take effect. Any saved deep sea entities will be destroyed at the next startup
When generating, the interval in seconds in between each entity spawn (island, ghost ship and floating city). Increase if you're experiencing lag when the deep sea is opening.
Number of floating city structures to spawn in the deep sea; increase for more urban content, but each adds significant entity count
Minimum distance in metres floating cities must be from the deep sea boundary edge when spawning
Minimum distance in metres required between floating city structures; prevents them overlapping when multiple are spawned
Radius in metres used when finding valid spawn positions for floating cities; prevents them spawning too close to map edges
0 Random, 1 North, 2 East, 3 South, 4 West
Number of ghost ship entities to spawn in the deep sea zone
Minimum distance in metres ghost ships must be from the deep sea boundary edge when spawning
Minimum distance in metres required between individual ghost ship entities
Exclusion radius in metres around each ghost ship used during spawn placement
Number of hackable crate entities to spawn in the deep sea zone as high-value loot targets
Number of small islands to spawn in the deep sea zone
Minimum distance in metres islands must be from the deep sea boundary edge when spawning
Minimum distance in metres required between individual island entities
Distance in metres from the main island terrain edge at which deep sea entrance portals are placed; affects how far offshore players must travel before encountering a portal
Exclusion radius in metres around each island used when finding valid spawn positions to prevent overlap
When enabled, outputs verbose deep sea system log messages (portal transitions, wipe events, entity moves) to the server log for debugging
Should the deep sea open as soon as the server wiped?
Number of RHIB patrol boat groups to spawn in the deep sea zone
Minimum distance in metres RHIB groups must be from the deep sea boundary edge when spawning
Minimum distance in metres required between individual RHIB group spawn points
Exclusion radius in metres around each RHIB group during spawn placement
When generating, the interval in seconds in between each spawn groups fill (dwellings/crates/scientists on island, ghost ships). Increase if you're experiencing lag when the deep sea is opening.
Maximum seconds before a deep sea re-opens after closing
Minimum seconds before a deep sea re-opens after closing
Duration in seconds of the deep sea wipe
Duration in seconds of the final wipe phase, bad weather kicking in etc
Seconds before radiation starts to ramp in before the deep sea wipe
Should the full server demos be deleted after they are uploaded
How many minutes between cleaning up demos from the disk
Max parallel requests when uploading demos
How much disk space full server demos can take before we start to delete them
Comma-separated list of player Steam IDs whose demos are automatically recorded on the server; empty means no targeted recording
Controls the behavior of recordlist, 0=whitelist, 1=blacklist
Directory to save full server demos
How often in seconds the server demo recording is flushed to disk (clamped 60-1800); more frequent flushes reduce data loss on crash
When enabled, the server records a full demo of all gameplay to disk; these can be uploaded or kept locally for replay and anti-cheat review
Maximum file size in megabytes before a demo recording is automatically split; prevents individual demo files from becoming unmanageably large
Maximum duration in seconds before a demo recording is automatically split into a new file; default is 3600 (1 hour)
Fraction of available upload bandwidth used when uploading demo files (0-1); lower values prevent demos from saturating the server connection
When enabled, completed server demo files are automatically uploaded to the configured UploadUrl endpoint after recording finishes
URL endpoint to which full server demo files are uploaded after recording; leave empty to disable demo uploads
Should we be zipping the demos before we upload them
Gets or sets the current in-game day of the month; used for calendar-driven events and date display
Gets or sets the current in-game month (1-12); used for seasonal event triggers and calendar display
When enabled, the server advances the in-game time of day automatically; disabling freezes time at its current value
Gets or sets the current in-game time of day as a decimal hour (0.0-24.0); writing a value immediately jumps the time to that point
Gets or sets the current in-game year; used for date display and long-running server event tracking
When enabled, this system is globally active; disable to deactivate the system for the current session
Read-only: reports whether Unity incremental garbage collection is enabled for this runtime; cannot be changed at runtime
Time slice in milliseconds allocated to incremental GC per frame; lower values reduce GC stutter but spread collection over more frames
When enabled a player wearing a gingerbread suit will gib like the gingerbread NPC's
Developer mode level: 0 = off, 1 = developer overlays and convar unlocks, higher values enable increasingly verbose debug logging
Disables the backpacks that appear after a corpse times out
When enabled, asset bundles are unloaded from memory after their assets are extracted, saving memory; disable to keep bundles resident
Number of Unity job worker threads; 0 or -1 sets the default (auto); higher values improve parallel job throughput on many-core CPUs
If a player sprays more than this, the oldest spray will be destroyed. 0 will disable
Maximum number of Unity job system worker threads; controls the background thread pool size for job dispatching
Controls the on-screen performance overlay detail level; 0 = off, higher values add more metrics such as FPS, ping, entity count, and memory usage
Base time (in seconds) that sprays last
Multiplier applied to SprayDuration if a spray isn't in the sprayers auth (cannot go above 1f)
When true, the server network position is updated to match the debug camera world position while spectating; useful for testing position-dependent server logic from the spectator view
Enables the Halloween event on the server, activating Halloween-themed loot spawns, NPC variants, and event-specific mechanics
Population active on the server, per square km
Modified damage from beancan explosion vs players (Default: 0.1).
Modifier to how much damage scarecrows take to the body. (Default: 0.25)
Stopping distance for destinations set while chasing a target (Default: 0.5)
The delay globally on a server between each time a scarecrow throws a beancan (Default: 8 seconds).
Population active on the server, per square km
Scarecrows can throw beancans (Default: true).
Disables all attire limitations, so NPC clothing and invalid overlaps can be equipped
When enabled, ownership tracking is applied to stackable items; disabled by default due to performance cost; servers can enable for full ownership auditing
Toggle printing time taken to send all global entities to client when they connect
When enabled, base entities are networked to all clients regardless of distance; disabling restricts base updates to players within the normal visibility radius
(default) true = only broadcast to clients with global networking enabled, false = broadcast to every client regardless
Toggle checking network group bounds whenever an entity changes its network group
When enabled, logs network visibility group changes to the server console, showing when entities enter or leave a player visibility radius
Network visibility radius in grid cells used in the deep-sea zone; kept smaller than overworld to limit underwater entity update overhead
Overrides the far network visibility radius in grid cells; -1 = use default; increase to send more distant entity updates to clients
Overrides the near (high-priority) network visibility radius in grid cells; -1 = use default
Maximum duration in seconds to batch clan chat messages to send to other servers on the nexus
Default distance between zones to allow boat travel, if map.contactRadius isn't set in the nexus (uses normalized coordinates)
URL endpoint to use for the Nexus API
How far away islands should be spawned, as a factor of the map size
Time in seconds to keep players in the loading state before going to sleep
When enabled, logs all Nexus zone transfer and communication events to the server console; useful for debugging cross-server player transfers
Scale of the map to render and upload to the nexus
Time in seconds to allow the server to process nexus messages before re-sending (requires restart)
Time in seconds to wait between server status pings
Interval in seconds to broadcast the player manifest to other servers on the nexus
Maximum time in seconds to keep transfer protection enabled on entities
Multiplier for nexus RPC timeout durations in case we expect different latencies
Shared secret key used to authenticate Nexus inter-server communication; must match across all servers in the same Nexus cluster
Time offset in hours from the nexus clock
Maximum amount of time in seconds that transfers should be cached before auto-saving
Name of the zone controller implementation used for this Nexus server (e.g. basic, advanced); controls how players are routed between servers
Maximum number of attempts the server makes to find a valid spawn point when spawning a party together; higher values increase the chance of grouping
Maximum distance in metres between party member spawn points when spawning a group together on wake-up
When enabled, party members respawn near each other rather than at random map locations when joining a server together
Cone angle in degrees of the patrol helicopter gun spread; higher values make the helicopter less accurate
Multiplier applied to all bullet damage dealt by the patrol helicopter; 1.0 = normal, 2.0 = double damage
Number of gun hardpoints active on the patrol helicopter; set to 0 to disable its guns without despawning it
How many minutes the patrol helicopter stays airborne before self-destructing; default is 30 minutes
When enabled, horses can be temporarily ragdolled by large physics impacts; disabling keeps horses upright during collisions
When enabled, players can be temporarily ragdolled by large physics impacts (e.g. explosions) before recovering; disabling keeps players standing
When enabled, Unity Physics auto-syncs transform changes to physics each frame; disable to manually control when transforms sync
When enabled, physics transform syncs are batched per frame for efficiency; disable to force immediate per-object sync
Minimum relative velocity at which a physics collision generates a bounce response; lower values cause more objects to bounce on light impacts
The collision detection mode that dropped items and corpses should use
Gravity multiplier
When enabled, logs ground-watch trigger events to the console, showing when players are detected as off the ground or falling through the world
Seconds between ground-watch checks on a player; lower values detect world-fall issues faster but increase CPU overhead
Number of consecutive ground-watch failures allowed before corrective action is taken on a player who appears to be falling through geometry
Send effects to clients when physics objects collide
The collision detection mode that server-side ragdolls should use
Do ragdoll physics calculations on the server, or use the old client-side system
Energy threshold below which a rigid body is put to sleep by the physics engine; lower values keep more objects awake, higher values reduce CPU usage
The default solver iteration count permitted for any rigid bodies (default 7). Must be positive
Maximum linear acceleration (m/s^2) that a vehicle towing joint can apply before the joint breaks; prevents unrealistic joint forces during towing
Do players and vehicles collide with trees?
When enabled, region ping estimates are automatically refreshed in the background to keep server list latency data up to date
When enabled, logs ping estimation results to the console; useful for debugging regional latency measurement accuracy
When enabled, the ping estimator collects latency samples to regional servers; disable to suppress background ping traffic
When enabled, ping estimation sends samples to all servers in parallel for faster results; uses more bandwidth simultaneously
Interval in minutes between automatic background ping estimation refreshes for server list region latency sorting
Number of ping samples collected per estimation cycle; more samples give a more accurate average latency but take longer to complete
When enabled, players drop their backpack as a loot bag when they die; disable to prevent backpack loot from appearing on death
When enabled, players drop their backpack when downed/wounded; disable to keep the backpack on the body until death or recovery
When enabled, tea/buff effects active on a player at the time of death are carried over to their next life instead of being lost
Whether the crawling state expires
When enabled, logs additional diagnostic information about pool hits, misses, and spills to the console
When enabled, this system is globally active; disable to deactivate the system for the current session
When enabled, object pools are pre-allocated at startup to avoid first-use latency; increases startup time but reduces runtime GC stutter
When enabled, object pools are pre-allocated at startup to avoid first-use latency; increases startup time but reduces runtime GC stutter
Interval in seconds between automatic exports of server profiler data to disk; lower values provide more frequent snapshots at a higher I/O cost
When enabled, the server native profiler runs in immediate mode, capturing every frame rather than sampling; more accurate but higher overhead
Main-thread allocation count threshold per tick for profiler notifications; 0 = disabled; helps isolate main-thread GC pressure
Main-thread memory usage threshold in kilobytes for profiler notifications; 0 = disabled
Allocation count threshold (per profiler tick) above which the native continuous profiler fires a notification; useful for detecting unexpected allocation spikes
Total managed memory threshold in kilobytes above which the native profiler fires a notification; helps detect memory leaks during long server runs
Worker-thread allocation count threshold per tick for profiler notifications; 0 = disabled; helps detect background task GC pressure
Worker-thread memory usage threshold in kilobytes for profiler notifications; 0 = disabled
Controls whether perfsnapshot commands emit chat messages
Prevents auto turrets getting added more than once to the IO queue
how long until something is considered hostile after it attacked
radius to check for other turrets
max interference from other turrets
How quickly can a turret acquire a potential target. Less is faster, but perf taxing
target everyone regardless of authorization
Set to false to disable the storage adaptor sorting functionality
When true, players can grab and swing on deployed vines; disable to prevent vine-swinging movement
Easy Anti-Cheat product ID used to authenticate this server with EAC services
Easy Anti-Cheat product key used alongside anticheatid for EAC server authentication
Sets the EOS (Epic Online Services) anti-cheat log verbosity level; higher values produce more detailed anti-cheat diagnostic output
When true clients must provide a valid EAC token to connect; disable only for testing or modded environments
Global multiplier for armour effectiveness against arrow and bow damage; 1.0 = default
Global multiplier for all arrow and bow damage dealt; 1.0 = default, 2.0 = double damage
Seconds an incoming connection has to complete authentication (Steam + EAC) before being forcibly disconnected
Automatically upload procedurally generated maps so that players download them (faster) instead of re-generating them
Automatically upload an image of the map, used to show the map in the server browser
HTTP API endpoint for centralized banning (see wiki)
Failure mode for centralized banning, set to 1 to reject players from joining if it's down (see wiki)
Timeout (in seconds) for centralized banning web server requests
Global multiplier for armour effectiveness against bleeding damage; 1.0 = default
Global multiplier for all bleeding damage over time; 1.0 = default
Server branch tag used by the server browser to identify modded or experimental variants; leave empty for vanilla
Global multiplier for armour effectiveness against bullet damage; 1.0 = default
Global multiplier for all bullet damage dealt by firearms; 1.0 = default, 2.0 = double damage
Allows backpack equipping while not grounded
Radius in metres within which a ceiling light provides artificial light that counts toward a growable plant's light requirement
Vertical offset in metres added when checking whether a ceiling light illuminates a plant directly below it
Censors the Steam player list to make player tracking more difficult
Seconds of delay before a combat event appears in the player's own combat log, preventing real-time tracking during a fight
Number of recent combat events retained in each player's combat log, viewable with combatlog
Will the in-game compass show at the top of the screen
How often (in seconds) composters advance their composting progress; default is every 300 seconds (5 minutes)
Enables or disables network packet compression on the server; compression reduces bandwidth at the cost of a small amount of CPU time
How often industrial conveyors attempt to move items (value is an interval measured in seconds). Setting to 0 will disable all movement
Seconds before a player corpse is removed from the world; default is 300 seconds (5 minutes)
Whether player corpses are spawned when players die; disabling removes corpses entirely and items are dropped directly
Do players go into the crawling wounded state
Maximum initial health given when a player dies and moves to crawling wounded state
Minimum initial health given when a player dies and moves to crawling wounded state
Duration of a full day/night cycle in seconds; default 500s means roughly one cycle every 8 real-world minutes
Seconds before environmental debris entities (e.g. broken barrel remnants) are removed from the world; default 30 seconds
Should the deep sea map be covered by fog of war
How much scrap is required to research default blueprints

default_raw: Rusticaland like the old days. SafeZone,TP , Market and more!
description: Short description of the server shown to players in the server browser
UsePlayerUpdateJobs 2 related - how many destroy messages to batch into 1 task
When enabled, debug spheres are drawn in the world showing the radiation zone boundaries during monument puzzle resets
Whether items drop to the ground from a player's inventory when they die; disable to prevent item drops on death
Whether or not to send additional analytics to EAC
Runs extra validation checks to prevent crashes and instead switch back to vanilla processing
Whether emoji ownership is checked server side. Could be performance draining in high chat volumes
Network encryption mode; 2 = enabled (recommended), 0 = disabled
Whether to check for illegal industrial pipes when changing building block states (roof bunkers)
Number of entities included in each entity-update batch per frame; tune to balance CPU time spent on entity processing
Time in seconds allocated to each entity batch update pass; the server will not start a new batch until this interval has elapsed
How many times per second entity network state is sent to clients; higher values reduce perceived lag for moving objects
none,primitive,medieval,frontier,rust
Enables automatic server-side game events such as helicopter patrols, airdrops and cargo ship visits
Lower damage of explosives to 1 and allow them to be triggered multiple times
Whether to run local avoidance for chickens, disabling might get a slight performance improvement but chickens will clip
Domain name to save when players favorite your server. The port can be omitted if using the default port or a SRV DNS record is created.
Override the root storage folder for server files; leave empty to use the default server/identity path
Should the in-game map be covered by a fog of war
How much area around the player is revealed when using fog of war. Must be a multiple of 32
Whether to run the food spoiling system
Maximum milliseconds per frame the server spends advancing food spoil timers; lower values reduce frame time impact at the cost of less frequent spoil updates
Short name of the game mode to activate on this server (e.g. 'softcore', 'hardcore'); applies convar overrides defined by that game mode's prefab
Multiplies crafting cost of firearm ammunition
URL of the banner/header image shown at the top of this server's page in the server browser
Should hte player see their direction on the map
Should the player see their position on the map
If true hot air balloons can be shot down with homing missiles
How long per frame to spend animating items moving into the hopper (will be instant if <= 0)
Server name displayed in the server browser
Maximum number of simultaneous outbound HTTP connections the server may have open at once; used for map uploads, ban checks, and other web requests
Unique identifier for this server instance; determines the subfolder used for saves, configs and map data
Minutes of in-game inactivity before a player is automatically kicked; set to 0 to disable idle kick
Minutes of inactivity before admin players are idle-kicked; 0 disables idle kick specifically for admins regardless of idlekickmode
Controls who is subject to the idle kick: 0 = nobody, 1 = non-admin players only, 2 = all players including admins
Base chance of recovery after incapacitated wounded state
Enables a faster way to move items around during conveyor transfers. Should be on unless there's a issue
How often industrial crafters attempt to craft items (value is an interval measured in seconds). Setting to 0 will disable all crafting
How long per frame to spend on industrial jobs
When enabled industrial transfers will abort if they start to take too long. Will lead to inconsistent splitting but should retain performance
IP address the server binds to; leave empty to bind to all interfaces
Maximum number of Steam server browser queries allowed per minute from a single IP address
Seconds before items dropped on the ground despawn; default is 300 seconds (5 minutes)
Upper cap on the loot container despawn multiplier; at default 24 the maximum lifetime is 24 x 5 min = 2 hours
Multiplier applied to the base item despawn time for items sitting inside loot containers; default 2x extends their lifetime
Fast-despawn time in seconds used for short-lived dropped items such as empty casings or small debris; default 30 seconds
Map level to load on startup, e.g. 'Procedural Map', 'Barren', or a custom map name
When true the server sends the map file to clients so they can load it without re-generating it locally
URL to download a custom map file from; if set the server will fetch and load this map instead of generating one
URL of the logo image shown for this server in the server browser
When enabled, long-distance ambient sounds (e.g. distant gunfire) are networked to clients; disabling may reduce bandwidth on busy servers
If no players are in this range kayaks, boogie boards and inner tubes will switch to a cheaper buoyancy system
Can players use the in-game map
Maximum byte size of the client info blob sent during the connection handshake; clients sending a larger payload are rejected
Maximum number of simultaneous connections allowed from the same IP address; helps mitigate connection-flooding attacks (clamped 1–1000)
Maximum total byte size of packets waiting for decryption; excess packets are dropped when the limit is reached
Maximum number of encrypted packets that can wait in the decryption queue before being dropped
Maximum milliseconds the decryption thread will block before timing out; increase if CPU-heavy encryption causes dropped packets (clamped 1–1000)
Maximum difference (in seconds) that two items with spoil timers can have and still be stackable
How many markers each player can place
How many pings can be placed by each player
How many stacks a single conveyor can move in a single tick
Maximum milliseconds the main game thread will wait for a network operation before timing out; increase to tolerate brief network stalls (clamped 1–1000)
Maximum byte size of a single console command packet from a client; oversized packets are rejected
Maximum byte size of a single global-entities network packet; oversized packets are dropped
Maximum byte size of a single global-trees network packet; oversized packets are dropped
Global cap on total network packets per second the server will accept across all connected clients combined
Maximum console command packets per second accepted from each client; rate-limits how quickly clients can send commands to the server
Maximum RPC (Remote Procedure Call) packets per second accepted from each client; limits how fast clients can trigger server-side actions
Maximum RPC signal packets per second accepted from each client; signal RPCs are lightweight event triggers used for interactions
Maximum sync-var (replicated variable) update packets per second accepted from each client
Maximum tick-update packets per second accepted from each client; these carry player inputs and must stay within this rate to be processed
Maximum voice chat packets per second accepted from each client; reducing this limits voice bandwidth usage per player
Maximum world-state update packets per second accepted from each individual client; prevents flooding the server with position spam
Maximum number of players allowed on the server at the same time
Maximum total byte size of the incoming read queue; excess bytes are dropped to prevent memory exhaustion from a flood of large packets
Maximum number of packets that can be queued in the incoming read queue; excess packets are dropped to prevent memory exhaustion
Maximum milliseconds the dedicated read thread will block waiting for incoming data before timing out (clamped 1–1000)
Maximum milliseconds the network receive thread is allowed to run per frame; increase if players report missed packets on high-population servers (clamped 10–1000)
Maximum number of unacknowledged network messages per connection before the server starts applying backpressure to that client
Maximum total byte size of the outgoing write queue; if the queue fills the oldest packets are dropped
Maximum number of packets that can be queued in the outgoing write queue; excess packets are dropped when the queue is full
Maximum milliseconds the dedicated write thread will block waiting to send data before timing out (clamped 1–1000)
Global multiplier for armour effectiveness against melee damage; 1.0 = default, values above 1.0 make armour stronger against melee
Global multiplier for all melee weapon damage dealt; 1.0 = default, 2.0 = double damage, 0.5 = half damage
Multiplier for player metabolism tick frequency; lower values slow down hunger, thirst and calorie consumption rates
Rate multiplier for status effect (buff/debuff) ticks; lower values slow all active modifiers such as poison, radiation sickness and warmth
Allows radiation to flood monuments to force puzzles to reset
Radiation damage per second applied to players inside a monument during its puzzle-reset radiation phase
Clamp radiation multiplier to this amount of meters, -1 = ignored
Force enable radiation in monument puzzles to confirm they work
How long does a monument puzzle need to be empty with full rads before it can reset
How long before the reset happens do we start applying radiation
Multiplier applied to a monument's normal radiation radius when the puzzle-reset radiation cloud is active; default 1.5x expands the zone beyond its usual boundary
When enabled, chat warnings are broadcast to players inside a monument shortly before its puzzle-reset radiation begins
Enables server-side network caching of entity state; when enabled only deltas are sent per update rather than the full entity data, significantly reducing bandwidth
Maximum byte size of the network entity cache; 0 means no hard limit
Enables low-level network activity logging on the server; produces verbose output useful for diagnosing connection and packet issues
Probability (0–1) per growth tick that a plant growing outside a planter box will die; default 0.005 means a 0.5% chance each tick
If two spoiled food items are both above this threshold then we will allow them to be stacked
All ammo drops from NPC loot will be multiplied by this
Marks this as an official Facepunch server; only set by Facepunch — do not enable on community servers
Radiation level at which the oil rig alarm triggers; 0 means the alarm activates immediately when any radiation is present

Multiplier for how long radiation lingers after an event at oil rig monuments; higher values extend the radiation duration
Soil saturation level (0–1) at which a planter box is considered perfectly watered for quality bonuses; default 0.6
Maximum milliseconds per frame spent processing furnace and campfire cook ticks; lower values keep frames smoother on high-population servers
Enables packet-type logging; must be true before the packetlog command will return data. Collects packet type call counts at runtime
How much to scale paintball damage when both the hit player and initiator player have paintball overalls on (see paintballstandarddamage for scaling standard paintball damage)
How much to scale standard paintball damage (see paintballoverallsdamage for scaling damage for when players have overalls on)
How long it takes to pick up a used parachute in seconds

When enabled, the puzzle reset timer pauses for monuments that have not been looted yet, preventing resets of untouched areas
Override the geographic region code used for ping estimation in the server browser; leave empty to use automatic detection
How long a ping should last
Enables plant light detection — growable plants will check nearby light sources each tick and adjust growth speed and quality accordingly
Multiplier for plant growth tick speed; values above 1.0 make plants grow faster, values below 1.0 slow growth
Size of the LRU player-state cache; higher values keep more player states in memory, reducing disk reads when reconnecting players
When true, fall damage is calculated server-side for improved anti-cheat security; disabling may reduce server load but allows clients to manipulate fall damage
Seconds of network inactivity before a connected player is timed out and disconnected
UDP port the server listens on for player connections

Maximum number of players to recheck at a time

Endpoint to use to check if players have premium status
Should F7 reports from players be printed to console
Enables PvE mode — players cannot damage other players; they can still be killed by NPCs and the environment

How much to increase time to kill in pvp globally, 2.0 = twice as long, 0.5 = half as long
How much to increase time to kill with melee in pvp globally, 2.0 = twice as long, 0.5 = half as long
Additional bullet damage multiplier applied only in player-vs-player combat, stacks with bulletdamage
Maximum number of Steam server browser queries the server will respond to per second before rate-limiting
UDP port used for Steam server browser queries; uses the game port if set to 0
Enables radiation zones at monuments; disabling removes all radiation hazards from the map

If set, this key will be included with any reports sent via reportsServerEndpoint (for validation)
Reposition attachments like storage adaptors if they have moved on reskins

Radius in metres around a player's death point — any sleeping bag or bed within this range is put on a respawn cooldown to prevent spawn-camping

Beds will increase their respawn time by this much
When a player respawns give them the loadout assigned to client.RespawnLoadout (created with inventory.saveloadout)
Minimum seconds that must pass after a player recovers from being wounded before they can be put into the wounded state again

Prints the current server decryption queue depth
Secondary salt value mixed into procedural world generation; used to vary monument and road placement

MS per frame to spend warming up entity save caches

How often (in seconds) the full entity schema is re-broadcast to all clients; default is every 1800 seconds
Seed value used for procedural world generation; changing this produces a completely different map layout
Persistent unique identifier for this server instance, used when recording demos and for analytics attribution
When enabled, weapons and tools holstered on a player's back are visible on their character model to other players
Skip death screen fade
UsePlayerUpdateJobs 2 related - how many snapshot messages to batch into 1 task
When true, vine tree variants are included during world generation; disable to remove all climbable vine trees from the map
Vertical eye-height offset in metres used when raycasting to determine whether a sprinkler can water a given plant
Enables structural stability simulation; when disabled buildings will not collapse even if their supports are destroyed
Enables automatic backups of server statistics data
Enables collection and reporting of gameplay statistics such as kill counts, damage dealt and resource gathered

When true players are kicked if Steam authentication fails; disable to allow connections when Steam auth servers are unreachable
Comma-separated server browser tag values (see wiki)
Number of server simulation ticks per second; higher values improve responsiveness but increase CPU usage
Controls whether the tutorial is enabled on this server
Number of entities processed per network update batch; lower values spread the load across more frames but increase total overhead
Number of entities processed per batch during the initial spawn network update; higher values send more entities per frame during map load
Server website URL displayed in the server browser; leave empty to show no link
When true, uses the old sprinkler initialisation process when loading a save; enable if upgrading from an older server version to avoid sprinkler layout issues
Opens a loot panel when interacting with a workbench instead of going straight into the tech tree. Designed for backwards compatibility with mods.
When enabled, plants that would normally be in a critically poor condition are given a minimum viable condition score instead of immediately dying
Enables per-player required system configuration checks on connect; allows different requirements to be enforced for individual players
Player Update parallelism mode: 0-3, Higher modes are faster but more experimental. 2 by default
Enables a server-wide required system configuration that all connecting clients must satisfy; used to enforce minimum hardware or software requirements
When transferring water, should containers keep 1 water behind. Enabling this should help performance if water IO is causing performance loss
How often (in hours) the water well NPC's update their sell orders
How long per frame to spend on updating water wheel power generation and water info
How much of a tax to apply to tech unlocks at a level 1 workbench. 10 = additional 10% scrap cost
How much of a tax to apply to tech unlocks at a level 2 workbench. 10 = additional 10% scrap cost
How much of a tax to apply to tech unlocks at a level 3 workbench. 10 = additional 10% scrap cost
Width and height of the procedurally generated world in metres; larger values create a bigger map with more resources
Maximum percent chance added to base wounded/incapacitated recovery chance, based on the player's food and water level
Base chance of recovery after crawling wounded state
Can players be wounded after receiving fatal damage
If set the loot spawn system will consider this the player count, not the actual player count. Useful for testing
Maximum population density scalar; controls the upper bound for how densely spawnable items fill their designated spawn areas at high player counts
Maximum spawn rate scalar applied to NPC/resource population spawning; the spawn tick rate scales up to this value as player count increases
Minimum population density scalar; controls the lower bound for how densely spawnable items fill their designated spawn areas at low player counts
Minimum spawn rate scalar applied to NPC/resource population spawning; lower values slow down respawn ticking when server population is low
Base player count used when computing population spawn rates; below this value player_scale group rates are not yet applied
Multiplier applied to group spawn rates based on current player count relative to player_base; higher values cause more group spawns as the server fills up
When scaling loot respawn rates by population, this will be considered the 'max' population, preventing loot speeding up if player counts are above this
When enabled, spawn groups (monument NPCs, timed event spawners) will respawn their entities after they are cleared
When enabled, individually tracked entities (e.g. specific persistent NPCs) will respawn after a delay when destroyed
When enabled, population spawners (animals, NPCs, resources) will respawn entities over time as they are killed or harvested
Interval in seconds between individual entity respawn ticks; controls how frequently the server checks for and respawns dead individual entities
Interval in seconds between population spawn ticks; lower values cause populations to refill faster but increase server CPU load
Floating-point tolerance used when comparing stability values during propagation; smaller values are more precise but can cause more recalculations
Stability value below which a building block is considered unsupported and will collapse; default 0.05 means blocks below 5% support fall
When enabled, logs when entities die because their ground support entity was destroyed or is missing
When enabled, logs each entity death caused by a stability collapse to the server console with position and prefab name
When enabled, logs every stability value change during propagation; very verbose, use only for targeted debugging
Maximum time in seconds that stability update jobs can run per server tick before being deferred to the next tick
Number of stability propagation iterations performed per tick; higher values resolve complex multi-block stability chains faster at the cost of CPU time
Maximum time in seconds that surrounding-support check jobs can run per server tick before deferral
Verbosity level for stability system logging; 0 = silent, higher values print more detail about stability calculations and propagation
The maximum amount physics ticks per frame on clients. If things are taking too long, time slows down
Desired physics ticks per second on clients
The maximum amount physics ticks per frame on the server. If things are taking too long, time slows down
Desired physics ticks per second on the server
When enabled, tree harvest events are broadcast to all connected clients, not just nearby players; useful for testing tree sync across the network

Distance in metres from a deep-sea portal boundary at which the repulsion force activates for vehicles
When enabled, wheel colliders are disabled on vehicles that have gone to sleep in the physics engine, reducing CPU overhead for parked vehicles
Determines whether trains stop automatically when there's no-one on them. Default: false
Determines whether vehicles drop storage items when destroyed. Default: true
Additional offset applied to the world boundary force zone, extending the buffer zone inside the boundary before the force ramps up
Distance in metres from the world boundary at which a repulsion force starts pushing vehicles back inward; prevents vehicles from leaving the playable area

Controls how wet surfaces become during snow; affects surface wetness shaders independently from rain wetness
Path to a world generation config file used by the procedural map generator; used when configString is empty
World generation config string passed directly to the procedural map generator; overrides the config file if set

Target number of gift entities to maintain per connected player during the xmas event; controls overall gift density on the server

Radius in metres around each player within which Christmas gift entities are spawned during the xmas event refill
Time in seconds before spawned prefabs are despawned. Set to 0 to disable despawning.
Time in seconds between prefab spawns. Set to 0 to disable spawning.
Variance in seconds to add/subtract from the spawn interval

If ai_dormant is true, any npc outside the range of players will render itself dormant and take up less resources, but wildlife won't simulate as well.
ai_dormant_max_wakeup_per_tick defines the maximum number of dormant agents we will wake up in a single tick. (default: 30)
ai_htn_animal_tick_budget defines the maximum amount of milliseconds ticking htn animal agents are allowed to consume. (default: 4 ms)
ai_htn_player_junkpile_tick_budget defines the maximum amount of milliseconds ticking htn player junkpile agents are allowed to consume. (default: 4 ms)
ai_htn_player_tick_budget defines the maximum amount of milliseconds ticking htn player agents are allowed to consume. (default: 4 ms)

If ai_htn_use_agency_tick is true, the ai manager's agency system will tick htn agents at the ms budgets defined in ai_htn_player_tick_budget and ai_htn_animal_tick_budget. If it's false, each agent registers with the invoke system individually, with no frame-budget restrictions. (default: true)
If an agent is beyond this distance to a player, it's flagged for becoming dormant.
If set to true the navmesh won't generate.. which means Ai that uses the navmesh won't be able to move
nav_obstacles_carve_state defines which obstacles can carve the terrain. 0 - No carving, 1 - Only player construction carves, 2 - All obstacles carve.
If true we'll wait for the navmesh to generate before completely starting the server. This might cause your server to hitch and lag as it generates in the background.

The maximum amount of nodes processed each frame in the asynchronous pathfinding process. Increasing this value will cause the paths to be processed faster, but can cause some hiccups in frame rate. Default value is 100, a good range for tuning is between 50 and 500.
If set to true, npcs will attempt to place themselves on the navmesh if not on a navmesh when set destination is called.

cover_point_sample_step_height defines the height of the steps we do vertically for the cover point volume's cover point generation (smaller steps gives more accurate cover points, but at a higher processing cost). (default: 2.0)

cover_point_sample_step_size defines the size of the steps we do horizontally for the cover point volume's cover point generation (smaller steps gives more accurate cover points, but at a higher processing cost). (default: 6.0)

Define cell size(in m) of a grid for trees - only has effect on world load and must be > 1. This affects how much data we send per tree cell(bigger the cell - more trees we have to send). The smaller the cell, the more cells we have to process and the more memory we need per player to track what's left to send(gridSize ^ 2 / 8 bytes). We readjust CellSize to ensure gridSize never exceeds 512.
When enabled, tree data is streamed to players based on proximity rather than sending all trees at connect; reduces initial bandwidth
Per-frame CPU budget in milliseconds allocated to sending tree streaming data per player
Total per-frame CPU budget in milliseconds for the tree streaming update system

Instead of reserializing grid cell on every tree add/removal(which can cost 0.25ms on 4.5k world), defer it to the streaming update. This reduces amount of times we need to serialize the tree list, but causes the player queue to take longer to process, as that's where evaluation happens.
When enabled, triggers use an exclude layer mask to filter out specific physics layers from trigger detection; toggling clears or sets all active triggers
When enabled, boat building station magnets are active and will magnetically attract compatible boat building blocks into position
Allow triggers to sleep if both they and their contents are stationary (TickMode 1 only)
world units a trigger can move in WS before it is woken
0 - old InvokeHandler, 1 - Jobs

Makes TriggerParentDelayedExit act as a TriggerParent again

Global multiplier applied to wildlife hazard reaction time; higher values make hazards slower to react to player presence

Number of days to add to the server's wipe timer during testing; registered as wipetimer.days_to_add_test; 0 = no adjustment
Number of hours to add to the server's wipe timer during testing; registered as wipetimer.hours_to_add_test
Custom cron expression for the wipe schedule. Overrides all other convars (except wipeUnixTimestampOverride) if set. Uses Cronos as a parser: https://github.com/HangfireIO/Cronos/
0=sun,1=mon,2=tues,3=wed,4=thur,5=fri,6=sat
Which hour to wipe? 14.5 = 2:30pm
The timezone to use for wipes. Defaults to the server's time zone if not set or invalid. Value should be a TZ identifier as seen here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones
Unix timestamp (seconds) for the upcoming wipe. Overrides all other convars if set to a time in the future.
